null) {\n        log.error(\"Async commit failed for offsets: \" + offsets, exception);\n        // Cannot retry automatically - must handle at application level\n    } else {\n        log.debug(\"Successfully committed offsets: \" + offsets);\n    }\n});\n\n// Continue processing without waiting for response\nConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\nfor (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n    process(record);\n}\n```\n\n**Asynchronous commit API:**\n\n```java\n// Commit without callback\nconsumer.commitAsync();\n\n// Commit with callback\nconsumer.commitAsync(new OffsetCommitCallback() {\n    @Override\n    public void onComplete(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets,\n                          Exception exception) {\n        // Handle response\n    }\n});\n\n// Commit specific offsets with callback\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets = new HashMap<>();\noffsets.put(tp0, new OffsetAndMetadata(100));\nconsumer.commitAsync(offsets, callback);\n```\n\n**Important semantics:**\n- Returns immediately (non-blocking)\n- Callback invoked asynchronously when response received\n- Callback exceptions do NOT propagate to `poll()` caller\n- Failed async commits are NOT automatically retried (application must retry)\n- Multiple pending async commits can race; last one wins\n- Useful for fire-and-forget scenarios but requires careful error handling\n\n### Sync vs. Async Trade-offs\n\n| Aspect | Synchronous | Asynchronous |\n|--------|------------|----------------|\n| Blocking | Yes, until response | No, returns immediately |\n| Error handling | Immediate exception | Callback receives exception |\n| Retries | Framework may retry | Application must retry |\n| Performance | Lower throughput (blocking) | Higher throughput |\n| Reliability | Stronger (confirms before proceeding) | Weaker (fire-and-forget) |\n| Use case | Exactly-once semantics | High-throughput, at-least-once |\n\n### Querying Committed Offsets\n\n```java\n// Query committed offset for specific partitions\nSet<TopicPartition> partitions = consumer.assignment();\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed = consumer.committed(partitions);\n\nfor (TopicPartition tp : partitions) {\n    OffsetAndMetadata offsetMeta = committed.get(tp);\n    if (offsetMeta != null) {\n        System.out.println(tp + \": offset=\" + offsetMeta.offset() +\n                          \", metadata=\" + offsetMeta.metadata());\n    } else {\n        System.out.println(tp + \": no committed offset\");\n    }\n}\n\n// With timeout\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed =\n    consumer.committed(partitions, Duration.ofSeconds(10));\n```\n\n**Behavioral notes:**\n- Queries broker's `__consumer_offsets` topic (coordinator)\n- Returns `null` for partitions with no prior commit\n- Includes metadata string and leader epoch if set\n- May block if coordinator discovery needed\n\n---\n\n## Flow Control and Position Management\n\n### Seeking to Specific Offsets\n\n```java\nTopicPartition tp = new TopicPartition(\"orders\", 0);\n\n// Seek to specific offset\nconsumer.seek(tp, 100);  // Next poll() fetches from offset 100\n\n// Seek to beginning\nconsumer.seekToBeginning(Arrays.asList(tp));  // Seek to offset 0\n\n// Seek to end\nconsumer.seekToEnd(Arrays.asList(tp));  // Seek to latest+1 (next to be produced)\n\n// Seek to offset from metadata (with leader epoch)\nOffsetAndMetadata offsetMeta = new OffsetAndMetadata(100, 2);\nconsumer.seek(tp, offsetMeta);\n\n// Validate offset was set\nlong position = consumer.position(tp);\nassert position == 100;\n```\n\n**Key semantic:** `seekToEnd()` seeks to the offset AFTER the last message. Reading from this position returns empty until new messages arrive.\n\n### Timestamp-Based Seeking\n\n```java\n// Search for offset by timestamp\nMap<TopicPartition, Long> timestampMap = new HashMap<>();\ntimestampMap.put(tp0, System.currentTimeMillis() - 3600000);  // 1 hour ago\ntimestampMap.put(tp1, System.currentTimeMillis() - 7200000);  // 2 hours ago\n\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> offsets =\n    consumer.offsetsForTimes(timestampMap);\n\nfor (Map.Entry<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> entry : offsets.entrySet()) {\n    TopicPartition tp = entry.getKey();\n    OffsetAndTimestamp offsetAndTimestamp = entry.getValue();\n\n    if (offsetAndTimestamp != null) {\n        consumer.seek(tp, offsetAndTimestamp.offset());\n        System.out.println(tp + \": Found offset \" + offsetAndTimestamp.offset() +\n                         \" at timestamp \" + offsetAndTimestamp.timestamp());\n    } else {\n        System.out.println(tp + \": No offset found for that timestamp\");\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Timestamp seeking requires:**\n- Broker-side log index retention (default enabled)\n- Messages must have timestamp (enabled by default)\n- Timestamp semantics: CreateTime (message creation) or LogAppendTime (broker append)\n\n### Beginning and End Offsets\n\n```java\n// Get beginning offset (oldest available)\nMap<TopicPartition, Long> beginningOffsets =\n    consumer.beginningOffsets(consumer.assignment());\n\n// Get end offset (offset after latest)\nMap<TopicPartition, Long> endOffsets =\n    consumer.endOffsets(consumer.assignment());\n\n// Example: Log all unread messages\nfor (TopicPartition tp : consumer.assignment()) {\n    long start = beginningOffsets.get(tp);\n    long end = endOffsets.get(tp);\n    System.out.println(tp + \": available offsets [\" + start + \", \" + end + \")\");\n}\n```\n\n**Retention semantics:**\n- Beginning offset: Oldest message still retained by broker\n- End offset: Offset of next message to be produced\n- Available messages: `[beginningOffsets, endOffsets)`\n- Older messages may be deleted due to retention policy\n\n### Position Queries\n\n```java\n// Get current position (offset of next record to fetch)\nlong position = consumer.position(tp);\nSystem.out.println(\"Next fetch will read from offset: \" + position);\n\n// After processing records, position advances\nConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\nlong newPosition = consumer.position(tp);\nSystem.out.println(\"Position advanced from \" + position + \" to \" + newPosition);\n\n// Querying position always updates (may contact broker)\nlong positionWithFresh = consumer.position(tp, Duration.ofSeconds(10));\n```\n\n**Position semantics:**\n- Advances automatically on `poll()` as records consumed\n- Can be queried at any time (except before assignment)\n- Position is ONE GREATER than highest offset consumed\n  - After consuming offset 99, position is 100\n  - Seek to 100 means next record fetched is offset 100\n\n### Pause and Resume\n\n```java\n// Pause specific partitions (stop fetching)\nconsumer.pause(Arrays.asList(tp0, tp1));\n\n// Records buffered before pause still returned on poll()\nConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n// No new fetch requests for tp0, tp1, but buffered records returned\n\n// Query paused partitions\nSet<TopicPartition> paused = consumer.paused();\nSystem.out.println(\"Paused: \" + paused);\n\n// Can still query position on paused partitions\nlong position = consumer.position(tp0);  // Works fine\n\n// Resume fetching\nconsumer.resume(Arrays.asList(tp0, tp1));\n```\n\n**Pause semantics:**\n- Stops NEW fetch requests to broker for paused partitions\n- Records buffered before pause still returned\n- Position and seeking work normally on paused partitions\n- Useful for downstream queue overflow handling (pause, wait for queue drain, resume)\n\n### Current Lag\n\n```java\n// Get lag for specific partition (lag = endOffset - position)\nOptionalLong lag = consumer.currentLag(tp);\n\nif (lag.isPresent()) {\n    System.out.println(\"Lag for \" + tp + \": \" + lag.getAsLong() + \" messages\");\n} else {\n    System.out.println(\"Lag unknown (position not yet initialized)\");\n}\n```\n\n**Lag calculation:**\n- `lag = endOffset() - position()`\n- Indicates how many messages behind the consumer is\n- Useful for monitoring consumer health and throughput\n\n---\n\n## Consumer Group Mechanics\n\n### Group Coordination Protocol\n\nKafka uses a group coordination protocol to manage distributed consumption:\n\n1. **Join**: Consumer joins group (initial `poll()` or `subscribe()` change)\n2. **Sync**: Partitions assigned to consumers based on assignor strategy\n3. **Assignment**: Broker sends assignment to each consumer\n4. **Stable**: Members consume in parallel\n5. **Rebalance**: Triggered by group change, partition count change, timeout\n6. **Revoke**: Partitions revoked from old owners\n7. **Assign**: Partitions assigned to new owners\n\n### Group ID\n\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, \"order-processing-group\");\n```\n\n**Semantics:**\n- Identifies the consumer group for coordination\n- Multiple consumers with same group ID form a group\n- Partitions distributed across group members\n- Each partition assigned to exactly one member at any time\n- Required for group-managed subscription (via `subscribe()`)\n- Not used for manually assigned consumers (via `assign()`)\n\n### Instance ID (Static Membership)\n\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, \"order-processing-group\");\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_INSTANCE_ID_CONFIG, \"consumer-1\");\n```\n\n**Benefits:**\n- Avoids rebalance during rolling restart\n- Consumer rejoins with same instance ID, keeps same partitions\n- Useful for avoiding processing interruptions\n- Requires `session.timeout.ms` to be large enough for restart\n\n### Group Metadata\n\n```java\n// Get current group membership metadata\nConsumerGroupMetadata groupMetadata = consumer.groupMetadata();\n\n// Use in transactional produce\n// (example with producer API)\nproducer.initTransactions();\nproducer.beginTransaction();\n\n// Process records and send results\nList<ProducerRecord<String, String>> results = process(records);\nfor (ProducerRecord<String, String> result : results) {\n    producer.send(result);\n}\n\n// Commit offsets transactionally\nproducer.sendOffsetsToTransaction(offsets, groupMetadata);\nproducer.commitTransaction();\n```\n\n**Use case:** Exactly-once semantics with transactional produce (coordinating consumer offsets with producer output)\n\n### Rebalancing Triggers\n\nRebalance occurs when:\n1. Consumer joins group (first `poll()` after `subscribe()`)\n2. Consumer leaves group (`close()`, `unsubscribe()`)\n3. Consumer times out (`max.poll.interval.ms` exceeded)\n4. Group membership changes (other members join/leave)\n5. Subscribed topic partitions change (count increases/decreases)\n6. User calls `enforceRebalance()`\n\n### Enforcing Rebalance\n\n```java\n// Request rebalance on next poll()\nconsumer.enforceRebalance();  // Default reason\n\n// With custom reason (for logging)\nconsumer.enforceRebalance(\"User requested rebalance due to external state change\");\n```\n\n**Use cases:**\n- Coordinating with external systems\n- Recovering from detected inconsistencies\n- Testing rebalance behavior\n\n---\n\n## Rebalancing and Callbacks\n\n### ConsumerRebalanceListener\n\nImplement to handle partition assignment changes.\n\n```java\npublic class MyRebalanceListener implements ConsumerRebalanceListener {\n    private KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer;\n    private DatabaseConnection db;\n\n    public MyRebalanceListener(KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer,\n                               DatabaseConnection db) {\n        this.consumer = consumer;\n        this.db = db;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n        // Called when about to lose partitions\n        // Safe to commit offsets here\n        System.out.println(\"Revoked: \" + partitions);\n\n        try {\n            consumer.commitSync();  // Commit pending offsets\n        } catch (CommitFailedException e) {\n            log.error(\"Commit failed during revocation\", e);\n        }\n\n        // Save any state/close resources\n        for (TopicPartition tp : partitions) {\n            db.savePartitionState(tp);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n        // Called when partitions assigned after rebalance\n        // Safe to initialize positions here\n        System.out.println(\"Assigned: \" + partitions);\n\n        // Restore saved state\n        for (TopicPartition tp : partitions) {\n            long savedOffset = db.getSavedOffset(tp);\n            if (savedOffset >= 0) {\n                consumer.seek(tp, savedOffset);\n            } else {\n                consumer.seekToBeginning(Arrays.asList(tp));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onPartitionsLost(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n        // Called when partitions lost without revocation\n        // (e.g., session timeout)\n        System.out.println(\"Lost: \" + partitions);\n        // Do NOT commit offsets - they're already owned by other consumers\n        // Clean up resources only\n    }\n}\n\n// Register listener\nconsumer.subscribe(Arrays.asList(\"orders\"), new MyRebalanceListener(consumer, db));\n```\n\n### Callback Execution Details\n\n**Execution context:**\n- Callbacks execute on consumer thread during `poll()` call\n- NO special threading; they block the polling thread\n- Exceptions propagate to `poll()` caller\n- Safe to call consumer methods inside callbacks (e.g., `commitSync()`, `seek()`)\n\n**Callback order (normal rebalance):**\n1. `onPartitionsRevoked()` - revoke old partitions\n2. [Network rebalance coordination]\n3. `onPartitionsAssigned()` - assign new partitions\n\n**Callback order (timeout/loss scenario):**\n1. `onPartitionsLost()` - lose partitions unexpectedly\n2. May not have `onPartitionsRevoked()` call\n\n**Protocol differences:**\n\n| Aspect | Classic (Eager) | Cooperative |\n|--------|-----------------|-------------|\n| All partitions revoked | Always (even if none assigned) | Only those being revoked |\n| `onPartitionsAssigned` receives | All newly assigned partitions | Only truly new partitions |\n| Rebalance duration | Longer (all stop) | Shorter (incremental) |\n| Suitable for | Simple cases | High-throughput (faster stabilization) |\n\n### Exception Handling in Callbacks\n\n```java\npublic class SafeRebalanceListener implements ConsumerRebalanceListener {\n    @Override\n    public void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n        try {\n            consumer.commitSync();\n        } catch (CommitFailedException e) {\n            // Not retriable from here - just log\n            log.error(\"Commit failed during revocation\", e);\n            // Continue anyway - revocation still happens\n        } catch (WakeupException e) {\n            // Can re-throw WakeupException if needed\n            throw e;\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n        // Exceptions here propagate to poll() caller\n        try {\n            restoreOffsetsFromDatabase(partitions);\n        } catch (DatabaseException e) {\n            // Wrap as KafkaException for consistency\n            throw new KafkaException(\"Failed to restore offsets\", e);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Important semantics:**\n- Throwing exception from callback does NOT prevent assignment change\n- Assignment is updated internally regardless of callback exception\n- Exception propagates to `poll()` caller\n- Re-throwing `WakeupException` or `InterruptException` is allowed\n\n---\n\n## Metadata and Monitoring\n\n### Topic Metadata\n\n```java\n// List all available topics\nMap<String, List<PartitionInfo>> allTopics = consumer.listTopics();\nfor (String topic : allTopics.keySet()) {\n    List<PartitionInfo> partitions = allTopics.get(topic);\n    System.out.println(topic + \": \" + partitions.size() + \" partitions\");\n}\n\n// Get partitions for specific topic\nList<PartitionInfo> partitions = consumer.partitionsFor(\"orders\");\nfor (PartitionInfo partition : partitions) {\n    System.out.println(\"  Partition \" + partition.partition() +\n                      \": leader=\" + partition.leader() +\n                      \", replicas=\" + partition.replicas().length);\n}\n```\n\n### Client Instance ID\n\n```java\n// Get unique client instance identifier (UUID)\nUuid clientId = consumer.clientInstanceId(Duration.ofSeconds(10));\nSystem.out.println(\"This consumer instance: \" + clientId);\n```\n\n### Metrics\n\n```java\n// Get all metrics\nMap<MetricName, ? extends Metric> metrics = consumer.metrics();\n\nfor (Map.Entry<MetricName, ? extends Metric> entry : metrics.entrySet()) {\n    MetricName name = entry.getKey();\n    Metric metric = entry.getValue();\n    System.out.println(name.name() + \" (\" + name.group() + \"): \" + metric.metricValue());\n}\n```\n\n**Common metrics:**\n- `fetch-latency-avg`, `fetch-latency-max` - Fetch request timing\n- `bytes-consumed-total`, `records-consumed-total` - Consumption statistics\n- `commit-latency-avg`, `commit-latency-max` - Commit timing\n- `assigned-partitions` - Current partition count\n- `lag-total` - Total lag across all partitions\n\n### Metric Registration\n\n```java\n// Register custom metric for subscription tracking\nKafkaMetric customMetric = new KafkaMetric() {\n    // Implementation...\n};\nconsumer.registerMetricForSubscription(customMetric);\n\n// Later, unregister\nconsumer.unregisterMetricFromSubscription(customMetric);\n```\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling\n\n### Exception Hierarchy\n\n```\nKafkaException (base for all Kafka exceptions)\n├── RetriableException (broker may retry)\n├── NonRetriableException (broker won't retry)\n└── (specific subtypes below)\n```\n\n### Common Exception Types\n\n#### CommitFailedException\n\n```java\ntry {\n    consumer.commitSync();\n} catch (CommitFailedException e) {\n    // Commit failed - consumer likely lost ownership of partitions\n    // Usually due to rebalance or timeout\n    log.error(\"Commit failed - may have lost partition ownership\", e);\n    // Cannot retry - state unknown\n}\n```\n\n**Causes:**\n- Consumer removed from group due to timeout\n- Rebalance occurred, partitions reassigned\n- Authorization failure\n- Broker-side error\n\n**Recovery:** Cannot retry from consumer side; rejoin group or restart.\n\n#### RetriableCommitFailedException\n\n```java\nconsumer.commitAsync((offsets, exception) -> {\n    if (exception instanceof RetriableCommitFailedException) {\n        // Safe to retry\n        log.warn(\"Retriable commit failure, will retry\");\n        consumer.commitAsync(offsets, this);  // Retry\n    } else if (exception != null) {\n        // Non-retriable error\n        log.error(\"Permanent commit failure\", exception);\n    }\n});\n```\n\n**Causes:**\n- Coordinator not available\n- Broker temporary failure\n- Request timeout\n\n**Recovery:** Can retry with backoff.\n\n#### WakeupException\n\n```java\n// In consumer thread\ntry {\n    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n    // ... process records ...\n} catch (WakeupException e) {\n    log.info(\"Consumer woken up\");\n    // Clean up and break polling loop\n    consumer.close();\n    System.exit(0);\n}\n\n// In another thread\nThread consumerThread = new Thread(() -> {\n    // ... polling loop ...\n});\nconsumerThread.start();\n\n// Later, wake up consumer\nconsumer.wakeup();  // Safe to call from any thread\nconsumerThread.join();\n```\n\n**Semantics:**\n- Only exception safe to throw from outside consumer thread\n- Interrupts blocking `poll()` without advancing offset\n- Must be caught and handled explicitly\n- Useful for graceful shutdown\n\n#### InterruptException\n\n```java\ntry {\n    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n} catch (InterruptException e) {\n    // Thread was interrupted (via Thread.interrupt())\n    log.info(\"Consumer thread interrupted\");\n    Thread.currentThread().interrupt();  // Re-interrupt\n}\n```\n\n**Semantics:**\n- Raised if thread is interrupted while blocked in `poll()`\n- Clears thread interrupt flag\n- Different from `WakeupException` (which is a KafkaException)\n\n#### OffsetOutOfRangeException\n\n```java\ntry {\n    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n} catch (OffsetOutOfRangeException e) {\n    // Committed offset is outside valid range [beginOffset, endOffset)\n    Set<TopicPartition> affectedPartitions = e.partitions();\n\n    // Recovery: seek to valid offset\n    for (TopicPartition tp : affectedPartitions) {\n        consumer.seekToEnd(Arrays.asList(tp));\n        // Or: consumer.seekToBeginning(Arrays.asList(tp));\n    }\n\n    // Retry poll\n    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n}\n```\n\n**Causes:**\n- Committed offset deleted due to retention policy\n- Topic was compacted\n- Committed offset was never valid\n\n**Recovery:** Seek to valid offset (beginning/end) and resume.\n\n#### NoOffsetForPartitionException\n\n```java\ntry {\n    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n} catch (NoOffsetForPartitionException e) {\n    // No committed offset for partition and auto.offset.reset=\"none\"\n    Set<TopicPartition> affectedPartitions = e.partitions();\n\n    // Recovery: manually seek to valid offset\n    for (TopicPartition tp : affectedPartitions) {\n        consumer.seekToBeginning(Arrays.asList(tp));\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Causes:**\n- First time consuming from partition (no prior commit)\n- `auto.offset.reset=\"none\"` configured\n- Committed offset was explicitly cleared\n\n**Recovery:** Seek to valid offset manually.\n\n#### RecordDeserializationException\n\n```java\ntry {\n    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n    // Records returned up to (but NOT including) bad record\n    for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n        process(record);\n    }\n} catch (RecordDeserializationException e) {\n    // Deserialization failed for record at e.offset()\n    TopicPartition tp = e.topicPartition();\n    long badOffset = e.offset();\n\n    log.error(\"Deserialization failed at \" + tp + \":\" + badOffset);\n\n    // Recovery: skip bad record and continue\n    long position = consumer.position(tp);  // = badOffset\n    consumer.seek(tp, badOffset + 1);  // Skip bad record\n}\n```\n\n**Important semantics:**\n- `poll()` returns records up to (not including) bad record\n- Position stops at bad record (allows inspection/skipping)\n- Consumer can skip bad record and resume\n- Useful for robustness (don't crash on bad data)\n\n#### RecordTooLargeException\n\n```java\ntry {\n    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n} catch (RecordTooLargeException e) {\n    // Record size exceeds fetch.message.max.bytes\n    // Cannot be fetched without increasing broker config\n    log.error(\"Record too large to fetch\", e);\n    // Manual recovery: admin API to inspect/delete offending record\n}\n```\n\n**Causes:**\n- Record larger than `fetch.message.max.bytes` (must match broker config)\n- Broker-side limit, cannot be overcome by client alone\n\n**Recovery:** Increase broker-side `replica.fetch.max.bytes` and `message.max.bytes` (requires broker restart).\n\n#### AuthorizationException\n\n```java\ntry {\n    consumer.subscribe(Arrays.asList(\"orders\"));\n} catch (AuthorizationException e) {\n    // User not authorized to read from topic\n    log.error(\"Not authorized to read from topic\", e);\n}\n```\n\n**Causes:**\n- User/principal lacks read permission\n- ACL not configured correctly\n- SASL authentication failed\n\n**Recovery:** Check security configuration and user permissions.\n\n---\n\n## Configuration-Driven Behaviors\n\n### Auto-Commit Behavior\n\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.ENABLE_AUTO_COMMIT_CONFIG, \"true\");\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.AUTO_COMMIT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG, \"5000\");  // 5 seconds\n```\n\n**Semantics:**\n- Auto-commit fires during `poll()` call\n- Commits highest offset consumed so far (in each partition)\n- Interval is minimum time between commits\n- Last commit may not fire if consumer closes abruptly\n- NOT triggered on `close()` alone\n\n**Example timeline:**\n```\nT=0:   poll() - no auto-commit (first poll)\nT=1:   poll() - no auto-commit (< 5 sec)\nT=5.1: poll() - auto-commit fires BEFORE returning records\nT=10:  close() - does NOT trigger auto-commit\n```\n\n### Offset Reset Policy\n\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.AUTO_OFFSET_RESET_CONFIG, \"earliest\");  // or \"latest\", \"none\"\n```\n\n**\"earliest\":** Start from beginning of partition\n- Useful for initial data load\n- May read old data\n\n**\"latest\":** Start from end of partition (skip historical data)\n- Useful for tailing new events\n- Misses historical data\n\n**\"none\":** Throw `NoOffsetForPartitionException` if no offset found\n- Useful for explicit offset management\n- Fails fast if offset undefined\n\n### Session Timeout\n\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG, \"45000\");  // 45 seconds\n```\n\n**Semantics:**\n- Broker removes consumer from group if no heartbeat for this duration\n- Triggers rebalance\n- For manual assign (via `assign()`), not used\n\n**When exceeded:**\n- Broker considers consumer dead\n- Other members may begin processing its partitions\n- `onPartitionsLost()` may be called\n- Next `poll()` may throw exception\n\n### Max Poll Interval\n\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.MAX_POLL_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG, \"300000\");  // 5 minutes\n```\n\n**Semantics:**\n- Broker requires `poll()` call at least once per this duration\n- Longer than `session.timeout.ms`\n- For group-managed consumers (via `subscribe()`)\n\n**When exceeded:**\n- Broker removes consumer from group\n- Triggers rebalance\n- Consumer loses partition ownership\n- Next `poll()` may throw `IllegalStateException`\n\n**Use case:** Detecting consumer hangs/deadlocks\n\n### Max Poll Records\n\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.MAX_POLL_RECORDS_CONFIG, \"500\");\n```\n\n**Semantics:**\n- `poll()` returns at most this many records across all partitions\n- Applies to a single `poll()` call, not cumulative\n- Useful for bounding processing batches\n- Affects `poll()` throughput vs latency tradeoff\n\n### Fetch Size Configuration\n\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.FETCH_MIN_BYTES_CONFIG, \"1024\");  // 1 KB\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.FETCH_MAX_WAIT_MS_CONFIG, \"500\");  // 500 ms\n```\n\n**Semantics:**\n- Broker waits up to `FETCH_MAX_WAIT_MS` to accumulate `FETCH_MIN_BYTES`\n- Larger `FETCH_MIN_BYTES` = fewer requests, higher latency\n- Smaller `FETCH_MIN_BYTES` = more requests, lower latency\n- Trade-off: throughput vs latency\n\n### Isolation Level\n\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.ISOLATION_LEVEL_CONFIG, \"read_committed\");  // or \"read_uncommitted\"\n```\n\n**\"read_uncommitted\" (default):**\n- Returns all messages (committed and in-progress transactions)\n- Highest throughput\n- May read aborted transactions\n\n**\"read_committed\":**\n- Filters out in-progress transaction messages\n- Stops at LSO (Last Stable Offset)\n- Ensures only committed messages consumed\n- Lower throughput due to filtering\n\n**Use case:** Reading committed transaction output.\n\n---\n\n## Usage Examples\n\n### Basic Consumer Loop (Auto-Commit)\n\n```java\nimport org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.*;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer;\nimport java.time.Duration;\nimport java.util.Properties;\nimport java.util.Collections;\n\npublic class BasicConsumer {\n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        Properties props = new Properties();\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, \"localhost:9092\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, \"my-group\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.ENABLE_AUTO_COMMIT_CONFIG, \"true\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.AUTO_COMMIT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG, \"1000\");\n\n        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);\n        consumer.subscribe(Collections.singleton(\"orders\"));\n\n        try {\n            while (true) {\n                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100));\n                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n                    System.out.printf(\"offset = %d, key = %s, value = %s%n\",\n                        record.offset(), record.key(), record.value());\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            consumer.close();\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Manual Offset Commit (Exactly-Once Pattern)\n\n```java\npublic class ExactlyOnceConsumer {\n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        Properties props = new Properties();\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, \"localhost:9092\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, \"my-group\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.ENABLE_AUTO_COMMIT_CONFIG, \"false\");  // Manual commit\n\n        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);\n        consumer.subscribe(Collections.singleton(\"orders\"));\n\n        try {\n            while (true) {\n                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n\n                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n                    // Process record\n                    processOrder(record.value());\n                }\n\n                // Only commit after ALL records processed\n                if (records.count() > 0) {\n                    try {\n                        consumer.commitSync();\n                        System.out.println(\"Committed \" + records.count() + \" records\");\n                    } catch (CommitFailedException e) {\n                        System.err.println(\"Commit failed: \" + e.getMessage());\n                        // Log, alert, but don't retry (state unknown)\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            consumer.close();\n        }\n    }\n\n    static void processOrder(String orderData) {\n        // Simulate processing\n        System.out.println(\"Processing: \" + orderData);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Rebalance Listener with State Management\n\n```java\npublic class StatefulConsumer {\n    static class SaveOffsetsOnRebalance implements ConsumerRebalanceListener {\n        private KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer;\n        private OffsetStore offsetStore;\n\n        SaveOffsetsOnRebalance(KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer,\n                              OffsetStore offsetStore) {\n            this.consumer = consumer;\n            this.offsetStore = offsetStore;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n            // Save offsets before losing partitions\n            Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsetsToCommit = new HashMap<>();\n            for (TopicPartition tp : partitions) {\n                offsetsToCommit.put(tp, new OffsetAndMetadata(\n                    consumer.position(tp),\n                    \"Saved at \" + System.currentTimeMillis()\n                ));\n            }\n\n            try {\n                consumer.commitSync(offsetsToCommit);\n                System.out.println(\"Saved offsets for \" + partitions);\n            } catch (CommitFailedException e) {\n                System.err.println(\"Failed to save offsets: \" + e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n            // Restore offsets when gaining partitions\n            for (TopicPartition tp : partitions) {\n                OffsetAndMetadata offsetMeta = offsetStore.fetch(tp);\n                if (offsetMeta != null) {\n                    consumer.seek(tp, offsetMeta);\n                    System.out.println(\"Restored \" + tp + \" to \" + offsetMeta.offset());\n                } else {\n                    consumer.seekToBeginning(Collections.singleton(tp));\n                    System.out.println(\"No saved offset for \" + tp + \", starting from beginning\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        Properties props = new Properties();\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, \"localhost:9092\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, \"my-group\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.ENABLE_AUTO_COMMIT_CONFIG, \"false\");\n\n        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);\n        OffsetStore offsetStore = new MyOffsetStore();  // Custom implementation\n\n        consumer.subscribe(\n            Collections.singleton(\"orders\"),\n            new SaveOffsetsOnRebalance(consumer, offsetStore)\n        );\n\n        try {\n            while (true) {\n                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n                    processOrder(record);\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            consumer.close();\n        }\n    }\n\n    static void processOrder(ConsumerRecord<String, String> record) {\n        System.out.println(\"Processing: \" + record.value());\n    }\n}\n\ninterface OffsetStore {\n    OffsetAndMetadata fetch(TopicPartition tp);\n    void save(TopicPartition tp, OffsetAndMetadata offset);\n}\n\nclass MyOffsetStore implements OffsetStore {\n    private Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets = new HashMap<>();\n\n    @Override\n    public OffsetAndMetadata fetch(TopicPartition tp) {\n        return offsets.get(tp);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void save(TopicPartition tp, OffsetAndMetadata offset) {\n        offsets.put(tp, offset);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Manual Assignment (Standalone Consumer)\n\n```java\npublic class StandaloneConsumer {\n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        Properties props = new Properties();\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, \"localhost:9092\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        // Note: NO group.id needed for standalone consumer\n\n        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);\n\n        // Manually assign specific partitions\n        TopicPartition tp0 = new TopicPartition(\"orders\", 0);\n        TopicPartition tp1 = new TopicPartition(\"orders\", 1);\n        consumer.assign(Arrays.asList(tp0, tp1));\n\n        // Seek to specific starting positions\n        consumer.seek(tp0, 100);\n        consumer.seek(tp1, 200);\n\n        try {\n            int messagesRead = 0;\n            while (messagesRead < 1000) {\n                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n                    System.out.printf(\"%s: %s%n\", record.key(), record.value());\n                    messagesRead++;\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            consumer.close();\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Async Offset Commit with Retry\n\n```java\npublic class AsyncCommitWithRetry {\n    static class RetryableOffsetCommitCallback implements OffsetCommitCallback {\n        private KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer;\n        private int retries;\n\n        RetryableOffsetCommitCallback(KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer) {\n            this.consumer = consumer;\n            this.retries = 0;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onComplete(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets,\n                              Exception exception) {\n            if (exception == null) {\n                System.out.println(\"Commit succeeded for offsets: \" + offsets);\n                return;\n            }\n\n            if (exception instanceof RetriableCommitFailedException && retries < 3) {\n                retries++;\n                System.out.println(\"Retriable failure, retrying... (attempt \" + retries + \")\");\n                consumer.commitAsync(offsets, this);  // Retry\n            } else {\n                System.err.println(\"Permanent or too many retries: \" + exception.getMessage());\n                // Log and move on - cannot safely retry\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        Properties props = new Properties();\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, \"localhost:9092\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, \"my-group\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.ENABLE_AUTO_COMMIT_CONFIG, \"false\");\n\n        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);\n        consumer.subscribe(Collections.singleton(\"orders\"));\n\n        try {\n            while (true) {\n                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n\n                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n                    System.out.println(\"Message: \" + record.value());\n                }\n\n                // Async commit with retry callback\n                if (records.count() > 0) {\n                    consumer.commitAsync(new RetryableOffsetCommitCallback(consumer));\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            // Final sync commit before closing\n            consumer.commitSync();\n            consumer.close();\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Error Handling and Recovery\n\n```java\npublic class RobustConsumer {\n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        Properties props = new Properties();\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, \"localhost:9092\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, \"my-group\");\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n        props.put(ConsumerConfig.AUTO_OFFSET_RESET_CONFIG, \"earliest\");\n\n        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);\n        consumer.subscribe(Collections.singleton(\"orders\"));\n\n        try {\n            while (true) {\n                try {\n                    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n\n                    for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n                        try {\n                            processOrder(record);\n                        } catch (RecordDeserializationException e) {\n                            // Bad record - skip it\n                            System.err.println(\"Skipping bad record at \" + e.offset());\n                            consumer.seek(e.topicPartition(), e.offset() + 1);\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    consumer.commitSync();\n\n                } catch (OffsetOutOfRangeException e) {\n                    System.err.println(\"Offset out of range, seeking to end\");\n                    consumer.seekToEnd(e.partitions());\n\n                } catch (NoOffsetForPartitionException e) {\n                    System.err.println(\"No committed offset, seeking to beginning\");\n                    consumer.seekToBeginning(e.partitions());\n\n                } catch (WakeupException e) {\n                    System.out.println(\"Shutdown requested\");\n                    break;\n\n                } catch (KafkaException e) {\n                    System.err.println(\"Kafka error: \" + e.getMessage());\n                    // Log and continue\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            consumer.close();\n        }\n    }\n\n    static void processOrder(ConsumerRecord<String, String> record) {\n        // Simulate processing that may fail\n        System.out.println(\"Processing: \" + record.value());\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Best Practices and Patterns\n\n### 1. Always Close Consumers\n\n```java\n// BAD: Connection leak if exception occurs\nKafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);\nconsumer.subscribe(topics);\nConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\nconsumer.close();\n\n// GOOD: Try-with-resources (Java 7+)\ntry (KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props)) {\n    consumer.subscribe(topics);\n    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n    // auto-closed\n}\n\n// GOOD: Try-finally\nKafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);\ntry {\n    consumer.subscribe(topics);\n    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n} finally {\n    consumer.close();\n}\n```\n\n### 2. Handle Rebalancing Gracefully\n\n```java\n// GOOD: Implement ConsumerRebalanceListener\nconsumer.subscribe(topics, new ConsumerRebalanceListener() {\n    @Override\n    public void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n        // Commit pending offsets before losing partitions\n        try {\n            consumer.commitSync();\n        } catch (CommitFailedException e) {\n            log.error(\"Failed to commit during rebalance\", e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n        // Restore state for newly assigned partitions\n        // Can use consumer.seek() or query external offset store\n    }\n});\n```\n\n### 3. Choose Appropriate Offset Commit Strategy\n\n**Auto-commit (default):** Use when at-least-once semantics acceptable\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.ENABLE_AUTO_COMMIT_CONFIG, \"true\");\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.AUTO_COMMIT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG, \"1000\");\n// Simple but risky for critical data\n```\n\n**Manual sync commit:** Use for exactly-once semantics\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.ENABLE_AUTO_COMMIT_CONFIG, \"false\");\n// Process records\nconsumer.commitSync();  // Blocks until broker confirms\n```\n\n**Manual async commit:** Use for high throughput with at-least-once\n```java\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.ENABLE_AUTO_COMMIT_CONFIG, \"false\");\nconsumer.commitAsync((offsets, exception) -> {\n    if (exception != null) log.error(\"Commit failed\", exception);\n});\n```\n\n### 4. Graceful Shutdown\n\n```java\npublic class ConsumerRunnable implements Runnable {\n    private final KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer;\n    private final AtomicBoolean shutdown = new AtomicBoolean(false);\n\n    public ConsumerRunnable(KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer) {\n        this.consumer = consumer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void run() {\n        try {\n            consumer.subscribe(Collections.singleton(\"orders\"));\n\n            while (!shutdown.get()) {\n                try {\n                    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records =\n                        consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n\n                    for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n                        processRecord(record);\n                    }\n\n                    consumer.commitSync();\n                } catch (WakeupException e) {\n                    if (!shutdown.get()) throw e;\n                }\n            }\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            log.error(\"Error in consumer loop\", e);\n        } finally {\n            consumer.commitSync();\n            consumer.close();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void shutdown() {\n        shutdown.set(true);\n        consumer.wakeup();  // Wake up blocked poll()\n    }\n\n    private void processRecord(ConsumerRecord<String, String> record) {\n        // Implementation\n    }\n}\n\n// Usage\nConsumerRunnable consumerRunnable = new ConsumerRunnable(consumer);\nThread consumerThread = new Thread(consumerRunnable);\nconsumerThread.start();\n\n// Later, shutdown gracefully\nconsumerRunnable.shutdown();\nconsumerThread.join();\n```\n\n### 5. Use Pause/Resume for Backpressure\n\n```java\nBlockingQueue<ConsumerRecord<String, String>> queue = new LinkedBlockingQueue<>(100);\n\n// Consumer thread: pause when queue full\nwhile (true) {\n    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n\n    for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n        try {\n            queue.put(record);\n        } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n            Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n            break;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Check queue capacity and pause if needed\n    if (queue.size() > 80) {  // 80% of 100\n        TopicPartition tp = new TopicPartition(record.topic(), record.partition());\n        consumer.pause(Collections.singleton(tp));\n    }\n}\n\n// Worker threads: resume when queue drains\nnew Thread(() -> {\n    while (true) {\n        try {\n            ConsumerRecord<String, String> record = queue.take();\n            processRecord(record);\n\n            if (queue.size() < 20) {  // Resume when < 20% full\n                Set<TopicPartition> paused = consumer.paused();\n                if (!paused.isEmpty()) {\n                    consumer.resume(paused);\n                }\n            }\n        } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n            Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n            break;\n        }\n    }\n}).start();\n```\n\n### 6. Monitoring and Metrics\n\n```java\nScheduledExecutorService scheduler = Executors.newScheduledThreadPool(1);\n\nscheduler.scheduleAtFixedRate(() -> {\n    // Print metrics every 10 seconds\n    consumer.metrics().forEach((name, metric) -> {\n        if (name.name().contains(\"lag\") || name.name().contains(\"committed\")) {\n            System.out.printf(\"%s: %s%n\", name, metric.metricValue());\n        }\n    });\n}, 0, 10, TimeUnit.SECONDS);\n\n// Shutdown scheduler\nscheduler.shutdown();\nscheduler.awaitTermination(10, TimeUnit.SECONDS);\n```\n\n### 7. Testing with MockConsumer\n\n```java\n@Test\npublic void testConsumerProcessing() {\n    MockConsumer<String, String> consumer = new MockConsumer<>();\n    TopicPartition partition = new TopicPartition(\"orders\", 0);\n\n    // Simulate partition assignment\n    consumer.assign(Collections.singleton(partition));\n    consumer.updateBeginningOffsets(Collections.singletonMap(partition, 0L));\n\n    // Add test records\n    consumer.addRecord(new ConsumerRecord<>(\n        \"orders\", 0, 0, \"order-1\", \"{ \\\"id\\\": 1 }\"\n    ));\n    consumer.addRecord(new ConsumerRecord<>(\n        \"orders\", 0, 1, \"order-2\", \"{ \\\"id\\\": 2 }\"\n    ));\n\n    // Test consumption\n    ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n    assertEquals(2, records.count());\n\n    // Simulate offset commit\n    consumer.commitSync();\n    assertTrue(consumer.committed(Collections.singleton(partition)).containsKey(partition));\n}\n```\n\n### 8. Separate Threading Models\n\n```java\n// GOOD: Single-threaded consumer, multi-threaded processing\nExecutorService executor = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(10);\n\ntry (KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props)) {\n    consumer.subscribe(topics);\n\n    while (running) {\n        ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n\n        for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n            // Submit to thread pool (don't call consumer methods from executor threads)\n            executor.submit(() -> processRecord(record));\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nexecutor.shutdown();\nexecutor.awaitTermination(10, TimeUnit.SECONDS);\n```\n\n**Key constraint:** Only call consumer methods from consumer thread. Use queues to communicate with processing threads.\n\n---\n\n## Summary of Key Behavioral Semantics\n\n1. **Thread Safety:** NOT thread-safe except `wakeup()`. All operations must occur on a single thread.\n\n2. **poll() Semantics:**\n   - Returns immediately if records available or timeout expires\n   - Can block longer than timeout if rebalance callbacks execute\n   - Handles heartbeats, metadata refresh, rebalance coordination\n\n3. **Offset Commits:**\n   - Auto-commit fires during `poll()` BEFORE records returned to user\n   - Sync commit blocks until broker response\n   - Async commit returns immediately, callback invoked later\n   - Position advances independently of commits\n\n4. **Rebalancing:**\n   - Triggered by group/subscription changes, timeouts, or explicit call\n   - Occurs during `poll()` calls\n   - Callbacks execute: revoke → rebalance → assign\n   - State updates despite callback exceptions\n\n5. **Position vs Committed:**\n   - Position: offset of next record to fetch (auto-advances on poll)\n   - Committed: last durable offset (set via commit)\n   - Committed should = next offset to read after restart\n\n6. **Error Recovery:**\n   - OffsetOutOfRange: seek to valid offset\n   - NoOffsetForPartition: seek to beginning/end or use auto.offset.reset\n   - RecordDeserializationException: position stops at bad record (can skip)\n   - CommitFailedException: cannot retry (state unknown)\n\n7. **Wakeup Semantics:**\n   - Only exception safe to call from outside consumer thread\n   - Interrupts blocking poll() without advancing offset\n   - Used for graceful shutdown\n\n8. **Group Management:**\n   - max.poll.interval.ms: enforce frequent polling\n   - session.timeout.ms: detect dead consumers\n   - Heartbeat separate from polling (sent in background)\n\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

It handles:\n\n- **Cluster membership**: Automatically maintains TCP connections to broker nodes\n- **Broker failure recovery**: Transparently adapts when brokers fail or partition leaders change\n- **Consumer group coordination**: Works with the Kafka broker's group coordination protocol for distributed, fault-tolerant consumption\n- **Offset management**: Tracks read position and supports automatic or manual offset commits\n- **Load balancing**: Partitions are automatically distributed across group members\n\n### Key Characteristics\n\n- **NOT thread-safe**: All operations must occur on a single thread\n- **Resource-intensive**: Maintains broker connections; must be explicitly closed\n- **Event-driven**: Uses `poll()` as the main entry point for all operations\n- **Transparent rebalancing**: Consumer group membership changes trigger automatic rebalancing via `poll()`\n\n---\n\n## Core Types\n\n### KafkaConsumer<K, V>","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}
